---
title: "Embedding Claude Artifacts in Your Blog"
description: "A guide to embedding interactive Claude artifacts directly in Quarto blog posts and books."
date: "2026-04-03"
categories: [ai, tutorial, claude]
---

## What Are Claude Artifacts?

Claude artifacts are interactive HTML/CSS/JS components generated by Claude.
They can be charts, calculators, visualizations, games, or any self-contained
web application.

## How to Embed Them

There are three ways to add Claude artifacts to your posts:

### 1. Inline HTML Artifact

Paste artifact HTML directly into a `.claude-artifact` div:

````markdown
::: {.claude-artifact}
<div id="my-widget">
  <h3>Interactive Widget</h3>
  <p>This is an inline artifact.</p>
</div>

<style>
  #my-widget { padding: 1rem; background: #1e293b; border-radius: 8px; }
</style>
````

::: {.claude-artifact}
<div style="padding: 1.5rem; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b, #283548); border-radius: 12px; text-align: center; font-family: system-ui;">
  <h3 style="color: #38bdf8; margin: 0 0 0.5rem 0;">Hello from Claude!</h3>
  <p style="color: #94a3b8; margin: 0;">This is an inline artifact rendered directly in the page.</p>
</div>
:::

### 2. External HTML File

Save a Claude artifact as an `.html` file and embed it with an iframe shortcode:

````markdown
{{< claude-artifact file="my-artifact.html" height="400px" title="My Artifact" >}}
````

### 3. From a Saved Artifact File

Drop any self-contained HTML file from Claude into your post's folder, then
reference it:

```
blog/posts/my-post/
├── index.qmd          # Your post
├── calculator.html    # Claude artifact
└── chart.html         # Another artifact
```

Then embed:

````markdown
{{< claude-artifact file="calculator.html" height="500px" title="ROI Calculator" >}}
````

## Example: Interactive Counter

Here's a simple interactive artifact:

::: {.claude-artifact}
<div id="counter-app" style="padding: 1.5rem; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b, #283548); border-radius: 12px; text-align: center; font-family: system-ui;">
  <h3 style="color: #38bdf8; margin: 0 0 1rem 0;">Interactive Counter</h3>
  <div style="font-size: 3rem; color: #e2e8f0; margin: 1rem 0;" id="count-display">0</div>
  <div style="display: flex; gap: 0.5rem; justify-content: center;">
    <button onclick="updateCount(-1)" style="padding: 0.5rem 1.5rem; background: #334155; color: #e2e8f0; border: 1px solid #475569; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.1rem;">−</button>
    <button onclick="updateCount(0)" style="padding: 0.5rem 1.5rem; background: #334155; color: #e2e8f0; border: 1px solid #475569; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.1rem;">Reset</button>
    <button onclick="updateCount(1)" style="padding: 0.5rem 1.5rem; background: #38bdf8; color: #0f172a; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 600;">+</button>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
let count = 0;
function updateCount(delta) {
  count = delta === 0 ? 0 : count + delta;
  document.getElementById('count-display').textContent = count;
}
</script>
:::

## Tips

- **Self-contained**: Artifacts should include all CSS/JS inline — no external dependencies
- **Responsive**: Use relative units and flexbox/grid so artifacts work on mobile
- **Dark theme**: Use the site's color palette (`#0f172a`, `#1e293b`, `#38bdf8`) for consistency
- **Height**: Set an appropriate `height` when using the iframe shortcode
